text: There was no US regular trading session on the report date, Saturday, June 20, 2026. Both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq are closed on weekends, and their regular core trading hours run Monday through Friday, 9:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. Eastern time, so no US cash equities traded on June 20.

market_session: US equity market holiday immediately preceding the report-date weekend
text: The calendar day immediately before the report-date weekend, Friday, June 19, 2026, was a full US market holiday for Juneteenth National Independence Day. The NYSE holiday calendar lists Juneteenth on Friday, June 19, and the Nasdaq US holiday schedule lists June 19, 2026 as Closed. As a result, the last US regular session predates both the holiday and the weekend.

market_session: US regular session close on 2026-06-18 (last session before the Juneteenth holiday and the weekend)

calculation.method: reported close and daily move from AP closing-index ledger

text: The most recent US regular session was Thursday, June 18, 2026, which closed higher across the board: the S&P 500 rose 80.48 points (1.1%) to 7,500.58; the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 72.15 points (0.1%) to 51,564.70; the Nasdaq Composite rose 496.28 points (1.9%) to 26,517.93; and the Russell 2000 rose 61.78 points (2.1%) to 2,979.77. These are carried as the standing levels into the long Juneteenth weekend, not a report-date print.

market_session: weekly change for the holiday-shortened trading week ended Thursday 2026-06-18
text: The trading week was shortened by the Friday Juneteenth holiday and effectively ended at Thursday's close. For that week, the S&P 500 rose 0.9% (69.12 points), the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.7% (362.44 points), the Nasdaq Composite rose 2.4% (629.09 points), and the Russell 2000 rose 1.2% (35.77 points).

market_session: year-to-date through the last regular session, 2026-06-18
text: Year-to-date through the June 18 close, the S&P 500 is up 9.6% (655.08 points), the Dow Jones Industrial Average is up 7.3% (3,501.41 points), the Nasdaq Composite is up 14.1% (3,275.94 points), and the Russell 2000 is up 20.1% (497.86 points).

market_session: next US regular session after the Juneteenth holiday weekend
text: US equity markets next open for regular trading on Monday, June 22, 2026, the first session after the Juneteenth holiday and the weekend.

market_session: next scheduled US equity-market holiday after the Juneteenth weekend
text: After the Juneteenth weekend, the next scheduled US equity-market closure is Friday, July 3, 2026, when both the NYSE and Nasdaq observe Independence Day (observed).
